policy.creation_rule: Create or edit a skill only when a router or workflow eval failure proves missing procedural knowledge.

skills.description: Classify incoming user requests before planning, repository traversal, tool selection, or implementation.
Define the problem, clarify scope, capture assumptions, containment needs, priority, resources, goals, and stop conditions before context gathering or execution planning.
Identify likely logic entry points for a scoped behavior, workflow, endpoint, command, class, function, test failure, documentation path, or refactor target.
Build a bounded context-gathering plan from scoped objectives, selected entry points, and follow-up context needs before impact mapping or execution planning.
Create bounded curated relationship lookup requests for code_context.lookup when a task needs callers, callees, imports, or module dependency context.
Explain a named function, class, or file from bounded code evidence without planning edits or running tools.
Identify direct and indirect tests related to a named behavior from bounded investigation evidence.
Select safe pytest validation commands with rationale from bounded evidence and known test files.
Determine whether a named behavior exists using bounded evidence while avoiding overclaimed absence.
Summarize callers and usages from bounded code_context.lookup relationship evidence grouped by file.
Explain where a configuration key or environment variable is defined or used from bounded evidence.
Summarize pasted test failure output into observed facts, likely cause, and next bounded inspection.
Locate endpoint, route, WebSocket, or message-handler source lines for a named request using bounded evidence.
Locate the source of an error, exception, log, logger, or printed message from bounded exact-text evidence.
Summarize a module or file from bounded AST and snippet evidence, including responsibilities, definitions, related tests, and gaps.
Locate data models, database table schemas, dataclasses, fields, or columns from bounded source evidence.
Locate imports and module dependencies for a requested file or module using code_context.lookup dependency evidence.
Identify test coverage gaps for a requested behavior from bounded read-only evidence, including covered tests, uncovered source areas, verification commands, and evidence gaps.
Find documentation for a requested behavior from bounded read-only evidence, including doc files, source refs, and documentation gaps.
Locate CLI or script entrypoints such as main modules, __main__ guards, and runnable commands from bounded read-only evidence.
Explain the runtime effect of a configuration key or environment variable from bounded read-only evidence.
Summarize recent commits, git status, changed files, and non-git limitations from read-only local repository evidence.
Diagnose a failing test from pasted output and bounded evidence with a read-only safe fix plan.
Summarize a behavior flow across multiple files using bounded investigation evidence and related tests.
Summarize dependency impact, risk level, related tests, and validation commands from bounded evidence.
Build smallest, medium, and broad validation tiers with covered-risk rationale from bounded evidence.
Diagnose a runtime error, exception, or stack trace from bounded read-only evidence.
Map a request, message, or data flow across bounded source evidence without editing files.
Compare two candidate code paths from bounded read-only evidence and state risks and gaps.
Identify the minimal safe change surface for a requested behavior change without implementation.
Draft a small unit-test proposal for an explicit configuration default value without mutating source files.
Draft a small unit-test proposal for an exact error or log message assertion without mutating source files.
Draft a bounded test assertion update from exact old assertion text to exact new assertion text.
Build a read-only impact map from scoped objectives, selected entry points, bounded context plans, and completed context results before execution-plan-writer.
Write a deterministic execution plan artifact from scoped problem details, entrypoint findings, bounded context plans, and impact evidence before implementation packets or repository mutation.
Convert approved execution-plan design_packet steps into bounded implementation packet candidates for the existing implementation.workflow.
Select controller-compatible pytest verification commands, manual checks, and coverage gaps for execution plans or implementation packet candidates.
Convert workflow results, validation output, plan reviews, failed test sessions, or founder/tester critique into structured follow-up records.
Locate background jobs, scheduled workers, sweepers, or periodic runtime tasks.
Locate pytest fixtures, test setup, and test data related to a named behavior.
Find API reference documentation, sample payloads, and contract files for a named request.
Find agent-facing invariant, policy, or handoff documentation for a named behavior.
Locate authentication, authorization, permission, or access-control checks for a named behavior.
Locate where a named behavior mutates in-memory state, indexes, caches, or persistent records.
Locate external API, exchange, webhook, SDK, or service integration points for a named behavior.
Locate exception handling, retry, fallback, or failure-path logic for a named behavior.
